cards.name: United Gateway Card
cards.headline: No-annual-fee United card with a light mileage boost on travel and gas.
cards.description: A starter United card for travelers who want MileagePlus earnings and basic airline perks without paying an annual fee.

rewards.notes: 2X miles on United purchases.
2X miles at gas stations.

signUpBonuses.displayDescription: Plus an additional 10,000 bonus miles after adding an authorized user in the first 3 months.

cards.name: United Quest Card
cards.headline: Mid-tier United card with stronger airline perks and bonus mile earning.
cards.description: A better fit than Gateway if you fly United regularly and can use the card's travel credits, bags, and award-flight perks.

rewards.notes: 3X miles on eligible United purchases.

cards.name: United Club Card
cards.headline: Premium United card built around lounge access and richer airline perks.
cards.description: Best for frequent United flyers who can justify the fee through United Club access, checked bags, and partner credits.

rewards.notes: 4X miles on United purchases.
2X miles on travel purchases outside United.

cards.name: Ink Business Premier Credit Card
cards.headline: High-spend business cashback card that pays extra on large purchases.
cards.description: A strong match for businesses with large-ticket expenses that can make the 2.5% tier and welcome offer worthwhile.

rewards.notes: 2.5% cash back on purchases of $5,000 or more.

cards.name: United Business Card
cards.headline: Core United small-business card with checked bags, travel perks, and partner credits.
cards.description: A practical option for business owners who fly United often enough to use its bag benefits and recurring travel perks.

rewards.notes: 2X miles on eligible United purchases.

cards.name: United Club Business Card
cards.headline: Premium United business card centered on lounge access and airport perks.
cards.description: Designed for business owners who want United Club membership, checked bags, and premium airport treatment on United flights.

rewards.notes: Base card earnings plus enhanced airline benefits on United purchases.

cards.name: IHG One Rewards Traveler Credit Card
cards.headline: No-annual-fee IHG card with solid hotel earning and a few everyday bonus categories.
cards.description: A lighter-weight IHG card for travelers who want hotel points and occasional IHG stays without paying an annual fee.

rewards.notes: 5X points on eligible IHG hotel purchases with the card.

cards.name: IHG One Rewards Premier Business Credit Card
cards.headline: IHG business card with elevated hotel earning and an accessible annual fee.
cards.description: A strong hotel-business card if your company spends on travel and you can use the card's anniversary-night style value.

rewards.notes: 10X points on eligible IHG hotel purchases with the card.

cards.name: World of Hyatt Business Credit Card
cards.headline: Business Hyatt card with hotel rewards and flexible category acceleration.
cards.description: Best for businesses that can direct meaningful spend toward Hyatt stays and rotating top-spend categories.

rewards.notes: 4X points on Hyatt purchases with the card.
2X points in your top three eligible spend categories each quarter.

cards.name: Southwest Rapid Rewards Plus Credit Card
cards.headline: Entry Southwest card with low annual fee and current Companion Pass-style promo value.
cards.description: A lower-cost way to get into the Southwest ecosystem if you value anniversary points and occasional airline perks.

rewards.notes: 2X points on Southwest purchases.
2X points on the first $5,000 in combined gas and grocery purchases each year.
2X points on the first $5,000 in combined gas and grocery purchases each year.

signUpBonuses.displayHeadline: 20,000 bonus points plus promotional Companion Pass

cards.name: Southwest Rapid Rewards Premier Credit Card
cards.headline: Mid-tier Southwest card with anniversary points and broader airline value than Plus.
cards.description: A good fit for Southwest flyers who want more annual value and stronger ongoing perks than the entry card.

rewards.notes: 3X points on Southwest purchases.
2X points on the first $8,000 in combined grocery and restaurant spend each year.
2X points on the first $8,000 in combined grocery and restaurant spend each year.

signUpBonuses.displayHeadline: 30,000 bonus points plus promotional Companion Pass

cards.name: Southwest Rapid Rewards Priority Credit Card
cards.headline: Best Southwest personal card for frequent flyers who can use the richer perks.
cards.description: The strongest Southwest personal card if you want better seating perks, more anniversary value, and stronger airline earning.

rewards.notes: 4X points on Southwest purchases.

signUpBonuses.displayHeadline: 40,000 bonus points plus promotional Companion Pass

cards.name: Capital One VentureOne Rewards Credit Card
cards.headline: No-annual-fee Venture family card for simple travel rewards earning.
cards.description: Useful if you want flexible Capital One miles without paying an annual fee, and you do not need Venture-level earn rates.

rewards.notes: 5X miles on hotels and rental cars booked through Capital One Travel.

cards.name: Capital One Venture X Business
cards.headline: Premium Capital One business travel card with strong lounge and portal value.
cards.description: Built for businesses that can justify the fee through premium travel perks, annual credits, and a large welcome offer.

rewards.notes: 10X on hotels and rental cars, and 5X on flights and vacation rentals, booked through Capital One Business Travel.

cards.name: Capital One Spark 2% Cash
cards.headline: Flat-rate business cashback card for owners who want a simple 2% everywhere.
cards.description: A clean business catch-all card if your company spends enough to outgrow no-fee 1.5% business options.

rewards.notes: 5% cash back on hotels and rental cars booked through Capital One Business Travel.

cards.name: Capital One Spark 1.5% Cash Select
cards.headline: No-fee business cashback card with uncomplicated flat-rate earning.
cards.description: A straightforward business card for owners who want unlimited cash back without tracking categories or paying an annual fee.

rewards.notes: 5% cash back on hotels and rental cars booked through Capital One Business Travel.

cards.name: Capital One Spark 2X Miles
cards.headline: Flexible-transfer business miles card with flat-rate everyday earning.
cards.description: A solid Capital One business travel card if you want transferable miles and stronger ongoing earn than a no-fee option.

rewards.notes: 5X miles on hotels and rental cars booked through Capital One Business Travel.

cards.name: Capital One Spark 1.5X Miles Select
cards.headline: No-fee business miles card with simple flat-rate travel rewards.
cards.description: A good entry business travel card for owners who want transferable Capital One miles without a yearly fee.

rewards.notes: 5X miles on hotels and rental cars booked through Capital One Business Travel.

cards.name: The Business Platinum Card from American Express
cards.headline: Premium Amex business travel card with heavy credits and airport-lounge focus.
cards.description: Best for high-spend business owners who can actually use the card's long list of travel and business credits.

rewards.notes: 5X Membership Rewards points on flights and prepaid hotels booked through AmexTravel.com.

cards.name: Blue Business Cash Card from American Express
cards.headline: Simple no-fee Amex business cashback card with elevated flat-rate earning.
cards.description: A straightforward choice for smaller businesses that want flat-rate cash back without tracking categories.

rewards.notes: 2% cash back on the first $50,000 in eligible purchases each calendar year, then 1%.

cards.name: Business Green Rewards Card
cards.headline: Lower-fee Amex business Membership Rewards card with travel-leaning earning.
cards.description: A lighter entry into Amex business points if you want Membership Rewards without jumping to Business Gold or Platinum.

rewards.notes: 2X points on AmexTravel.com purchases.

cards.name: Hilton Honors Business Card
cards.headline: Hilton business card with strong hotel earn, quarterly credits, and mid-tier status value.
cards.description: One of the better hotel-business cards if your company spends meaningfully with Hilton properties.

rewards.notes: 12X Hilton Honors points on eligible Hilton purchases.
5X points on other eligible purchases on the first $100,000 per year, then 3X thereafter.

cards.name: U.S. Bank Triple Cash Rewards Visa Business Card
cards.headline: No-fee business cashback card with elevated earn in practical operating categories.
cards.description: A strong small-business cash-back card for owners spending on fuel, restaurants, software, and recurring services.

rewards.notes: 3% cash back at gas and EV charging stations on transactions of $200 or less.
3% cash back with cell phone service providers.

cards.name: U.S. Bank Business Altitude Power Visa Signature Card
cards.headline: Premium-ish U.S. Bank business card with solid flat-rate points and travel-center upside.
cards.description: A good fit for businesses that want straightforward 2X points with some extra upside for travel-center and wallet spend.

rewards.notes: 2.5X points on up to $5,000 in combined mobile wallet and virtual card purchases each quarter.
6X points on prepaid hotels and car rentals booked directly in the Travel Center.

cards.name: Business Leverage Visa Signature Card
cards.headline: Business points card that automatically boosts your top spend categories.
cards.description: Useful for businesses with uneven monthly spend because the top-category bonus adjusts without manual tracking.

rewards.notes: 2X points in the top two categories where you spend the most each month automatically.
5X points on prepaid hotels and car rentals booked directly in the Travel Center.

cards.name: U.S. Bank Business Shield Visa Card
cards.headline: Low-fee-flexibility business card with a travel-center bonus and intro APR angle.
cards.description: More of an operating card than a pure rewards card, but still worth cataloging because it adds cash-flow utility and modest rewards.

rewards.notes: 5% cash back on prepaid air, hotel, and car rentals booked directly in the Travel Center.

cards.name: Business Advantage Customized Cash Rewards Mastercard credit card
cards.headline: Flexible Bank of America business cash-back card with a selectable bonus category.
cards.description: Strong for owners who want category choice on top of a no-fee business card and already bank with Bank of America.

rewards.notes: 3% cash back in the cardholder's selected category; gas and EV charging is the default category.

cards.name: Business Advantage Unlimited Cash Rewards Mastercard credit card
cards.headline: Simple flat-rate Bank of America business cash-back card.
cards.description: A clean fit for small businesses that want uncomplicated rewards and may qualify for Preferred Rewards for Business boosts.

cards.name: Business Advantage Travel Rewards World Mastercard credit card
cards.headline: No-fee Bank of America business travel card with flat-rate points and no foreign transaction fee.
cards.description: A simple travel-rewards business card for Bank of America shops that want points without an annual fee.

cards.name: Citi / AAdvantage Platinum Select World Elite Mastercard
cards.headline: Core American Airlines card for flyers who want miles, bags, and priority perks.
cards.description: A mainstream American Airlines co-brand card that rounds out a major gap in the current Citi lineup coverage.

rewards.notes: 2X miles on eligible American Airlines purchases.

cards.name: Citi / AAdvantage Executive World Elite Mastercard
cards.headline: Premium American Airlines card centered on lounge access and richer airline earning.
cards.description: Best for American Airlines loyalists who want Admirals Club-style access and are comfortable with a large annual fee.

rewards.notes: 4X miles on eligible American Airlines purchases.

cards.name: Citi / AAdvantage Business Card
cards.headline: American Airlines business card that fills a major co-brand business gap.
cards.description: A relevant addition for small-business travelers who want AAdvantage miles and business-focused airline perks.

rewards.notes: 2X miles on eligible American Airlines business purchases.

cards.name: Citi Strata Card
cards.headline: No-fee Citi ThankYou-style card with everyday bonus categories.
cards.description: A relevant missing Citi everyday rewards card now that the Strata family has expanded beyond Premier.

cards.name: SoFi Unlimited 2% Credit Card
cards.headline: Flat-rate fintech cashback card that deserves coverage alongside Robinhood and Apple.
cards.description: A sensible addition to the catalog because it represents a mainstream modern fintech catch-all card.

cards.name: PayPal Cashback Mastercard
cards.headline: Popular fintech cashback card with a boosted earn rate when checking out through PayPal.
cards.description: An important catalog add because it is one of the more visible mainstream online-shopping cashback cards.

rewards.notes: 3% cash back when checking out with PayPal.

cards.name: Fidelity Rewards Visa Signature Card
cards.headline: Well-known flat-rate 2% card tied to Fidelity accounts and investing workflows.
cards.description: A worthwhile addition because it is one of the better-known set-it-and-forget-it 2% cards in the U.S. market.

rewards.notes: 2% cash back when rewards are deposited into an eligible Fidelity account.

cards.name: Venmo Credit Card
cards.headline: High-visibility Venmo card with dynamic top-category cash-back earning.
cards.description: A mainstream social-payments card worth covering because it remains widely recognized and publicly available.

rewards.notes: 3% cash back on your top eligible spend category, 2% on the next, and 1% on all other eligible purchases.
